speaker: MATT
raw_text: They are the worst. You went and had a discussion at the Estate Sybaritic with Ophelia Mardun, of whom you were sent by The Gentleman to interface with and do a deed for her a little over two weeks ago. There it seemed that the intent of your job placed here in this city has aligned with your current goal of freeing your friends and seeking vengeance against the Iron Shepherds, as they currently work for a competitive family to the Mardun house, and in response to them destroying parts of the trade route that the Mardun family enjoys through the center and southern sides of the empire, they wish you to instill damage upon their traders as well, by eliminating the Iron Shepherds. You were given some ideas as to where you might find some help, you gathered your things, made your way north into the seemingly corrupted and haunted Savalierwood, to seek information about these Iron Shepherds and where they are. You narrowly avoided two large, immensely mutated, bone-spurred bear creatures. You sought out and cased the exterior of the Sour Nest, which is the fortress that was given to the Iron Shepherds by the family that pays them and keeps them in their employ. You decided to send your new druid friend, Nila, in the form of a small mouse, over the wall, delivery via owl. She snuck through some portions of the fortress to gather intel, and to get an idea of where you were going and what may reside within. A few close calls, and some discovery of what can be expected within the interior, you made your way back, safely, to join your friends.

It's like the color flares up once the light hits it and then seems to gray as it passes on. You now stand, temperature much warmer, and there's just that stone structure ahead.

Nott leaps, double flip in the air, lands on the opposite side, skids a bit as your feet hit a slight bit of the icy floor, but you manage to catch yourself, no issue.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Even still, you approach the outskirts of The Sour Nest. The clouds of the night are broken here and there, the very darkened night occasionally broken by the errant moonbeam that manages to sweep through the open grove where this fortress was built. The canopy itself cleared out, leaving it open for the occasional peek of white through the clouds. The same perimeter wall you see being slowly trained across by two guards dressed in the same hooded attire as those that you previously saw. As you saw as well bringing the three carts northward from the Glory Run Road. As you begin to approach, Keg, unfortunately, manages to step on a hornet's nest. It crunches under your foot, and, for a brief second, you inhale very sharply knowing this familiar sound-- This is not the first hornet's nest you've stepped into. Thankfully, a moment passes, no hornets. It was abandoned and had fallen from a tree. However, you do hear a voice on the wall say, "Hello?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do I know if Lorenzo is a loyal person, like would he give or would he just let someone die if they got taken?

preceding_turns.text: He's definitely a fan of an eye for an eye.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY

He shot an owl.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Shortly after midnight, you watch as two figures ascend from the inside of the wall climbing up different ladders that mark the entrance and exit. They both interface in a brief conversation, hushed voices, and then relieve the two previous guards, who then descend down on the inside of the courtyard via those same ladders and disappear into The Sour Nest fortress. So there has been a guard changeover now. It's past midnight, pushing one in the morning. What would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. No windows in the bottom floor. There is a front double door, and there is a single door in the back that you know leads into a kitchen.

preceding_turns.text: I'm not terrified of it, I'm just saying it could be terrifying. Hi, Frumpkin.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Shortly after midnight, you watch as two figures ascend from the inside of the wall climbing up different ladders that mark the entrance and exit. They both interface in a brief conversation, hushed voices, and then relieve the two previous guards, who then descend down on the inside of the courtyard via those same ladders and disappear into The Sour Nest fortress. So there has been a guard changeover now. It's past midnight, pushing one in the morning. What would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. No windows in the bottom floor. There is a front double door, and there is a single door in the back that you know leads into a kitchen.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go Charlotte, go! You're radiant!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He has three hit points, so I think he's actually a bigger spider than the one you're imagining. See, he's right there. He's very big.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How big is that spider?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a pretty big spider.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's three hit points worth of spider.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That looks like a three hit point spider.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Somebody could step on it and he could still go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a four or five hitter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Get Brian Foster in here with his ping pong racket.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's arguably terrifying, I'll give it to you. I was a little wrong, just a little.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I put it on Beau's shoulder.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm not terrified of it, I'm just saying it could be terrifying. Hi, Frumpkin.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About a minute passes before Beauregard pulls back away, this strange trance passing over her.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have kids?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Why are you badgering him with questions?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, he's a new friend and I talk when I'm nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Why don't you let him think and answer questions one at a time?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sure, well, I'm learning from-- I don't know who, actually. None of us are good at this. Nila, I guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nila is really good at this; she's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: What am I good at?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know I can only hear muffles. Choice words. I think they're talking about you, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: It's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I pull the haversack off and say  I have a decent idea, anyway. I think that you should use this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Much like how you spend a moment with your smell bag, you watch as Beauregard seems to focus into this strange, vibrant pink satchel or shoulder bag that Caleb has been carrying.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: What is inside?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's a long rest to come back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to wait until right after he passes, and then I want to do some ninja stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you just sipping tea?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: First, I need you to make a stealth check because you're having to move right up against the wall. This is a ten-foot clearing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Use the dodeca if you need to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both of you guys roll stealth checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Mmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't she get a plus ten to the stealth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is a plus ten to stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Then that gives me a 20 total. Okay, 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, how did I get the same thing? Look.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I cursed you!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need to stealth too?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Luck, you have luck still.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's your total?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Does that apply to her, the Pass without a Trace?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But you have a luck left.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I have two luck points left, but I feel like--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you have one because you used one last game.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: But we short rested, didn't we?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we rested.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. Going past the barrel pile where you had previously made your way in and out via owl the previous day. As you guys scoot past, you hear the front door of the facility (thudding) (creaking). It opens ever so faintly to the inside, and a figure peers out, having apparently heard something.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think it's as long as they're within 30 feet, it's like a field and you can say yea or nay. That's my view.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't say at the moment of casting you have to select; just that for the duration you choose. So, my interpretation, I would allow someone to step into the radius, and you could then choose.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we know better. We know you don't have to cast it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll snuggle up to Nila.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I choose Keg.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's tippy-toe around the back door.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, everyone who's now making their way around the back, make another stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Am I at disadvantage now? Still?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're still at disadvantage, because you still have your armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So two plus ten plus five. 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I miss Vax so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Plus ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Plus ten, whatever it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys begin to head around this direction?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (counting) That puts you right there. That ends your go. It is now Clay's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh Jesus. I will bonus action disengage, and run through the door that the spider told me to go through.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which one?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The closed one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you go to that door. It's locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Locked? Great, I'll go through the other door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can use your action to try and fast pick it, if you like. It's up to you. Because you can do that as a rogue.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, I'll do that. I'll fast pick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pick fast. 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12? It holds tight.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. I have any more movement?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have currently 20 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will run into the next room, as far as I can get.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so you have one minute before that spell goes away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. What's everybody else doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The gate's open.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I want to go down.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I feel like I want to stay with Caleb, in case.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so Keg is staying up with Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Is that cool with you, Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll go with you, Nila.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I can't hear anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nila, you head down.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm going to stand behind you, in case.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Come join us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll come down with you, that's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I feel you next to me?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, maybe I'll put my hand on you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I? If I feel the dwarf next to me, I would use a free hand to jam some licorice back into my mouth and start chewing furiously, anxiously. I'm so scared of everything. And cast Haste again, into the dwarf.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Tight.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That puts it over 20. Both of you together (strains), and one of the bars comes off on one side. He gets in quickly and puts his shoulder in and pushes against the other, and that bends off to the side, leaving just enough room for him to push through. It takes a minute and you're helping him and grabbing his elbow, and the two of your together-- You feel something pop in his shoulder, but it's just enough to get his body to wedge out of the space, and he comes back out of it and embraces you-- the other arm limply hanging at one side, but he takes you in his hand. He looks down at his son and looks over at the rest of you, tired. Still, amongst the trauma he has suffered, there is a pride and a silent strength to him-- very much similar to that you've seen in Nila. "I do not know by what grace you've come to us, but thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, they're safe. They came to help. Can you get out?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Help me."

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: How? Tell me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He stands up in the cage; his hunched form is crammed in by this cage that was not designed for his firbolg body. Nevertheless, he reaches out and grabs the bars where you had started. "On three: one, two, three-- (strains)." He's going to go ahead and make a strength check with advantage. That's a natural 17... and a natural 19! I love it when it works for the narrative! (laughing)
